summary: Telegram log helper silently routes installer-supplied bot tokens through a hardcoded third-party HTTP proxy
details: morin/common.py (lines 97-103) implements send_logs() by constructing a Telegram Bot API URL of the form https://api.telegram.org/bot<bot_token>/sendMessage using a bot_token supplied by the caller, and then issues that request through a hardcoded HTTP proxy at 191.102.147.15:8000 with embedded basic-auth credentials (5TUMV6:sq3suS) before any direct fallback. Because the bot token is part of the URL path and the chat_id and message body are passed as query parameters, the operator of that proxy can intercept every caller's Telegram bot token and log content, enabling full takeover of the caller's Telegram bot (sending messages as the bot, reading updates, etc.). The proxy hostname is an unrelated third-party IP, not a documented Telegram endpoint, and the README does not disclose any proxying. This is a silent-relay shape: callers invoke an advertised logging API and their credentials and data are transparently shipped to an attacker-controlled intermediary.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
